events.title: Jia Qianqian was a literary high-flier. Then came the online claims of plagiarism
events.summary: Jia Qianqian, daughter of internationally acclaimed writer Jia Pingwa, has become the latest high-profile figure in Chinese academia to fall from grace after a university investigation confirmed allegations of plagiarism. In a statement on Wednesday, Northwest University said its months-long investigation found that nine of Jia’s published papers contained plagiarised material. The university had terminated her teaching appointment and revoked her associate professor title. In addition, her 2009...

events.title: Seoul’s historical royal sites see 6.6% jump in foreign visitor numbers
events.summary: Gyeongbokgung Palace was the most visited site with 3.65 million visitors.

events.title: US strikes around Tehran for first time in most recent wave of attacks, says Iranian state media
events.summary: The US says it fired a missile into a Curaçao-flagged oil tanker's funnel after claiming the ship, which was sailing toward Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export terminal in the Persian Gulf, "ignored multiple warnings".

events.title: South Korea’s international adoptees seek justice, not homecoming
events.summary: South Korea's government has acknowledged wrongs, but advocates say accountability is still lacking.

events.title: Over 500 feared dead in two suspected shipwrecks off Myanmar: UN
events.summary: The two vessels departed from war-torn Myanmar’s Rakhine State in late June.

events.title: Doctor who damaged Japan temples, shrines by performing ‘anointings’ admits to own ‘delusion’
events.summary: The US-based man had reportedly performed "anointings" on shrines and temples across Japan since at least 2012.
